content:
✨ simutil | Dart
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a fast, terminal-based interface for developers to manage and launch Android emulators and iOS simulators, improving workflow efficiency.
✨ Key Features: • One-Key Launch for emulators/simulators • Custom Android launch options • Shutdown devices • Logcat viewing with filtering • Wireless connection to physical Android devices (ADB Tools)
📖 Summary: SimUtil is a cross-platform terminal UI application for launching, managing, and connecting to Android emulators and iOS simulators. It provides a streamlined interface within the terminal to interact with virtual devices, offering features like one-key launch, custom launch options, and wireless connectivity to physical devices.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 tabwrangler | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To declutter browser tabs, improve performance by closing inactive tabs, and provide easy restoration of closed tabs.
✨ Key Features: • Automatic tab closing • Tab Corral for managing and restoring closed tabs • Full session restore with browsing history • Tab Lock to prevent auto-closing • Website exclusion list
📖 Summary: Tab Wrangler is a browser extension for Chrome and Firefox that automatically closes unused tabs to improve browser performance and reduce clutter. It provides features to manage closed tabs, prevent specific tabs from closing, and exclude certain websites.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ iOS | Swift
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a dedicated, native mobile and desktop application for users to interact with their Home Assistant smart home setup on Apple devices.
✨ Key Features: • Native iOS, iPadOS, and macOS applications for Home Assistant • Control and monitor smart home devices • Manage automations and scenes • TestFlight beta program for early access • Integration with Apple's developer tools for building and testing
📖 Summary: This repository provides the official Home Assistant client application for Apple platforms, allowing users to control and monitor their smart home devices from their iPhones, iPads, and Macs. It offers a native experience for managing automations, scenes, and devices.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 IPBan | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Protecting servers (dedicated or cloud) on Windows and Linux from unauthorized access attempts by automatically blocking malicious IP addresses.
✨ Key Features: • Automatic IP banning based on failed login attempts • Support for Windows and Linux platforms • Monitors event viewer and log files for security events
📖 Summary: IPBan is a free, open-source security software for Windows and Linux that automatically blocks IP addresses of attackers attempting to gain unauthorized access to servers. It monitors event logs and application logs for failed login attempts and integrates with firewalls to ban malicious IPs, protecting servers from brute-force attacks and botnets.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
